---
# TEMPLATE — Reference (Diátaxis: information-oriented)
#
# Reference describes the machinery. Austere, exhaustive, factual. Reader is
# looking up an answer, not learning or building.
#
# For this project, the *primary* reference is generated by docgen and lives
# at <https://effect-graphql.js.org>. Use this template only when a Reference
# page is needed for something docgen can't emit — e.g. a JSON config schema,
# an environment-variable table, or a wire-format spec.
#
# Rules:
#   1. Title names the thing being described. No verbs.
#   2. Structure mirrors the thing (fields for a config; endpoints for an API).
#   3. Every entry has type, default (if any), and one-line description.
#   4. No prose paragraphs of context. Move that to Explanation.
#   5. No examples that teach; examples that clarify a specific field.
---

# <Thing name>

One sentence: what this reference documents.

## <Section — e.g. "Fields", "Options", "Endpoints">

### `field-name` — `type`

Default: `value` (if applicable).

One-line description of what this field controls or means. If needed, one
inline `code` example showing the value format.

### `next-field` — `type`

Default: `value`.

Same shape.

## <Next section, if the shape has multiple>

Same shape.
